Cognate with Old Uyghur [script needed] (alqïš, “praise, blessing”); Kumyk алгъыш (alğış, “blessing; expression of gratitude”), Southern Altai алкыш (alkïš, “blessing”), Uzbek olqish (“an act of wishing well, blessing, benediction; applause”), Uyghur ئالقىش (alqish, “applause; praise; benediction”), Turkish alkış (“applause”), Tuvan алгыш (algış, “blessing”), etc.

senses: appreciation (a just valuation or estimate of merit, worth, weight, etc.

etymology: Derived from Proto-Turkic *bog- (“to tie up, to strangle”). Cognate with Karakhanid [script needed] (boɣun, “knuckle”), Old Anatolian Turkish [script needed] (boğun, “joint, knot”); Bashkir быуын (bıwın, “joint; generation”), Kazakh буын (buyn, “joint; generation”), Uzbek boʻgʻin (“joint; generation”), etc.
From Proto-Common Turkic *yig ~ *yüy ~ *yüg. Cognate to Southern Altai јик (ǰik), Karachay-Balkar джик (cik), etc.

Uzbek qobiq (“skin, shell”),
Turkish kabuk (“shell”),
Tuvan хавык (xavık, “husk”),
Chuvash хупӑ (hup̬ă, “bark”), etc.
From Proto-Turkic. Influenced by Old East Slavic.

Tuvan арага (araga, “vodka; wine”),
Yakut арыгы (arïgï, “wine”), etc.
